nodes.title: Models Note
widgets_values:
Model loader :
  - Depending on whether you use a safetensors model such as fp8 or a GGUF model, Depending on whether you use a safetensors model such as fp8 or
  a GGUF model, In the "Fast Groups Bypasser" node, enable the either node. Enabling both "0.Safetensors_Models" and "0.GGUF_Models" will result 
  in an error, so set the one you want to use to "yes" and the one you don't to "no" depending on the models you use. The model is placed in the 
  following directory:    (Safetensors_Models)   ComfyUI/models/diffusion_models         (GGUF_Models)  ComfyUI/models/unet

Model Patch Torch Settings :
 - Setting "enable_fp16_accumulation" to "true" seems to reduce VRAM usage and improve processing speed. It seems to be more effective when used 
  together with the sage attention node. I don't know the details, so if you don't need it, set "Fast Groups Bypasser" to "no".

sage_attention : 
  - Sage attention improves processing speed. It is a little difficult to install. If you don't have it installed, set "Fast Groups Bypasser" to 
  "no."

Lightx2v LORA :
  - This is required for official models and models such as "smooth mix i2v v2.0". If you are using a model that already has lightx2v lora 
   merged, please bypass this. Each LORA is quite different, so it can be hard to decide which one is best, but if you're unsure, we 
   recommend the following LORA in Civitai.　https://civitai.com/models/1585622?modelVersionId=2337890

FFGO LORA : 
  - For more details, please check the GitHub page. In my opinion, a weight of 0.3 is effective enough. If it is too large, it may restrict 
   movement.　https://github.com/zli12321/FFGO-Video-Customization  

ModelSamplingSD3 :
  - 5 to 8 is recommended, but honestly you may not notice much difference. I've been using "5" lately, but I'm not sure.

nodes.title: Video Note
widgets_values:
RIFE VFI :
  - RIFE VFI is a frame interpolation node. It automatically creates frames between the frames of the original 16-frame video to smooth it out. Setting the "multiplier" value 
   to "2" doubles the frame rate to 32 fps, while setting it to "4" converts it to 64 fps. While multiples of 2 are considered good, this has not been verified. Generally, 
   32 fps is considered good for animation, and 64 fps for live-action. While VRAM is cleaned before this node, this node still puts a strain on VRAM, so please be aware of 
   this.

Upscale :
  - Use this if you want to upscale a 480p video to 720p. This uses a model, but you can replace it with your preferred node. However, the quality will be lower than if you 
   don't upscale, so if you have an environment where you can generate 720p from the beginning, we recommend not using upscale, even if it takes time.

nodes.title: Prompt Note
widgets_values:
Negative_Prompt :
  - Enter the negative prompt. When using Lightx2v LORA, the 
   CFG is set to "1", so the negative prompt seems to be 
   disabled. However, it can be reflected by introducing a 
   node called "NAG".

Positive_Prompt :
  - This node used in the positive prompt enables wildcards. 
   It is okay if you do not use wildcards. Enter the prompt 
   in the upper half of the node window.

nodes.title: Sampler Note
widgets_values:
Subgraph node :
  - Subgraph is a package of a part of a node. It is useful for organizing the entire workflow. You can view the contents by clicking the top right of the Subgraph node.

PainterI2V (Advanced) :
  - This is the key node in my videos. It's generally said to improve slow motion, but its effect on camerawork is even more important. It makes a camera that previously didn't
   move at all move surprisingly well. I use the successor node called "Advanced." I haven't confirmed whether this can be installed in ComfyUI Manager. I install it using git 
   clone. If you can't install it, the previous node will be sufficient. For more details, please see the following GitHub page.
   (PainterI2V)  https://github.com/princepainter/ComfyUI-PainterI2V          (PainterI2V Advanced)  https://github.com/princepainter/ComfyUI-PainterI2Vadvanced

WanVideoNAG :
  - NAG is introduced to reflect the contents of the negative prompt. When using Lightx2v LORA, CFG is set to "1", which disables the negative prompt. However, NAG seems to 
   consume VRAM, so you may want to bypass it depending on your PC environment. It is not clear how effective it will be.

CFGZeroStar :
  - I understand that CFGZeroStar was installed in the WAN2.1 era to roughly stabilize video. Like NAG, I installed it because I felt it had some effect, so you can bypass or 
   delete it if you like.

nodes.title: Input Note
widgets_values:
Fast Groups Bypasser (rgthree) :
  - This node bypasses nodes by group.
   Enabling both "0.Safetensors_Models" and "0.GGUF_Models" will 
   cause an error, so set the one you want to use to "Yes"
   and the one you don't want to use to "No."
   Also, if you're not using ”1.Lightx2v Lora"or"1.Patch Torch 
   Settings" or "1.Sage Attention," set them to "No." Using Sage
   attention can improve speed by about 30%, but this is limited to 
   NVIDIA GPUs and similar devices.
   This workflow can generate continuously only once. If you are 
   generating only once, turn on "1st Section" and "1st Video" and
   turn off "2nd Section". If you are generating twice and 
   combining the videos, turn on "1st Section" and "2nd Section" 
   and "2nd Video" and turn off "1st Video". If you want to use 
   "Upscale", turn it on. 

Seed (rgthree):
  - Use this if you want to fix the seed value. You can generate up to
   six times, with each generation having its own unique seed value.
   Normally, you should click "Randomize Each Time" so that "-1" is 
   displayed. Generation will be performed randomly. In that case, the 
   seed value for each generation will be displayed at the bottom. If 
   you want to fix the seed, click on the seed value field or enter 
   the seed value directly.
   For example, you can fix the firsgenerations, and then
   regenerate randomly the second generation.
   However, if you regenerate a first section, the final frame will 
   change, so you cannot fix second sections. 

Motion_Amplitude : 
  - This is the setting value for the painteri2v node. Between 
   1.0 and 1.5, the higher the value, the faster the movement 
   will be, but this also increases the risk of color shifts, 
   so I think "1.1" is sufficient. If you are generating intense 
   movements such as those for sports, try a higher value.

Frame_Rate :
  - Specifies the number of frames per second.
   The WAN2.2 14B model is trained with 16 frames, so 16 frames is 
   recommended, but you can change it to any number you like. 
   24 frames, etc., can also be used.

RIFE_VFI_Multiplier :
  - At the end of generation, specify the multiplier for frame 
   interpolation at the RIFE_VFI node. Setting it to "2" will result 
   in two frames of the specified number, while "4" will result in 
   four times the number. Incidentally, if you specify 16 frames per 
   second, "2" will result in 32 fps, and "4" will result in 64 fps. 
   I generally set it to four times the number.

1st & 2nd Second :
  - This is the number of seconds for the video to be generated. 
   Since it is based on 16fps, the number of frames is 
   automatically determined based on the number of seconds. 
   As you know, WAN2.2 is based on 5 seconds and 81 frames, 
   so exceeding this may cause a phenomenon called loopback or 
   a decrease in video quality. By the way, my PC specs limit is
   7 seconds, and exceeding this significantly impairs 
   processing speed. Also, the 1st and 2nd seconds can be 
   different. They are finally added together.

Total & split steps : 
  - Set the number of steps. "total" is the number of steps for 
   both High and Low, and "split" is the number of steps for 
   High only. When using Lightx2v LORA, a total of 4 to 8 steps 
   is generally recommended. Consider the balance between 
   generation time and quality.

Width & Height :
  - Set the video size. Generally, 480 x 832 (480p) or 720 x 
   1280 (720p) is used. Any number is fine, but a multiple of 
   16 is recommended. I often use 720 x 1072.

Power Lora Loader :
  - Set LORA. You can apply different LORAs to the first and 
   second generation. Using LORA is very important and has a big 
   impact on the generation results. You also need to give it an 
   appropriate weight. This will require a lot of trial and 
   error. As a tip for beginners, the ideal weight is the 
   smallest value that allows LORA to achieve the desired 
   movement. The higher the weight, the more it will affect the 
   quality.
